LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-28-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-25-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-12-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-23-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings: Declaration Of Corey M. Tolmasoff
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead claims under the Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act and the California Uniform Commercial Code. As explained below, the Song-Beverly Act claims are no longer viable (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Set For Trial On Nov-03-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
